sql: SELECT host, SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_op_count, SUM(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_op_count, AVG(CASE WHEN io_type = 'read' THEN bw END) AS avg_read_bw, AVG(CASE WHEN io_type = 'write' THEN bw END) AS avg_write_bw, SUM(bytes) AS total_bytes_combined FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, 'read' AS io_type, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS bytes, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) AS bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' UNION ALL SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, 'write' AS io_type, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS bytes, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) AS bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished') GROUP BY host ORDER BY total_bytes_combined DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the average and maximum latency, average transfer duration, average bandwidth, and total transfer count — revealing which destination sites suffer the highest network latency on inbound data paths and whether latency correlates with reported bandwidth?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS max_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY avg_latency DESC

question: For started JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average and maximum site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util, average core count requested, and total started job count — revealing the CPU pressure environment each site sustains when new jobs begin executing and whether highly parallel jobs tend to start under heavier load?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cores_requested, COUNT(*) AS started_job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_util DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events bucketed by total_queue_time (short: below 60 seconds, moderate: 60 to 600 seconds, long: 600 seconds and above), what is the average execution duration, average cost, average retry count, and total job count per bucket — revealing how pre-execution wait time correlates with job complexity, monetary cost, and failure rate?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) < 60 THEN 'short_queue' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) < 600 THEN 'moderate_queue' ELSE 'long_queue' END AS queue_tier, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY queue_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL))

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by host, what is the total bytes read, average and maximum disk read bandwidth, average read duration, and total read operation count — identifying which individual compute hosts sustain the greatest disk read pressure and whether per-host bandwidth varies significantly across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, COUNT(*) AS read_operation_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For JobAllocation events of both states grouped by site, what is the count of allocation starts, count of allocation completions, the completion ratio of finished to started, average site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util at completion, and average site_storage_util at completion — revealing how reliably allocations complete at each site and what multi-resource conditions accompany successful allocation finishes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, SUM(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Started' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS allocation_starts, SUM(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Finished' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS allocation_completions, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Finished' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Started' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS completion_ratio, AVG(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Finished' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) END) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Finished' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) END) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CASE WHEN STATE = 'Finished' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) END) AS avg_site_storage_util_at_finish FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' GROUP BY site ORDER BY completion_ratio DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by the source-to-destination site pair (route), what is the total transfer count, average and maximum transfer duration, total bytes transferred, average bandwidth, and average link_load — identifying the most congested and heavily trafficked inter-site data paths across the simulation grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY transfer_count DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by disk identifier and site, what is the total write operation count, average and maximum disk write bandwidth, total bytes written, average write duration, and average site_storage_util at write completion — revealing which individual storage disks carry the heaviest write workloads and operate under the greatest storage pressure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_write_bw, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk, site ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by disk identifier and site, what is the total read operation count, average and maximum disk read bandwidth, total bytes read, and average read duration — identifying which individual storage disks sustain the greatest read throughput and how per-disk bandwidth varies across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_read_bw, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk, site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the total job count, average retries per job, maximum retries on a single job, count of jobs requiring zero retries, count requiring exactly one retry, count requiring more than one retry, and the retry rate expressed as the fraction of jobs needing at least one retry — revealing site-level job reliability and which sites systematically suffer the highest failure and re-execution rates?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS zero_retry_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS one_retry_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS multi_retry_jobs, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS retry_rate FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY retry_rate DESC

question: For each site, what is the average, minimum, and maximum simulation-time gap between JobAllocation completion and JobExecution start for the same job, along with the total job count — revealing the per-site staging or scheduling latency that separates resource allocation from the onset of actual compute execution?
sql: SELECT json_extract(exec_start.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(exec_start.TIME - alloc_done.TIME) AS avg_staging_delay, MIN(exec_start.TIME - alloc_done.TIME) AS min_staging_delay, MAX(exec_start.TIME - alloc_done.TIME) AS max_staging_delay FROM EVENTS alloc_done JOIN EVENTS exec_start ON alloc_done.JOB_ID = exec_start.JOB_ID WHERE alloc_done.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND alloc_done.STATE = 'Finished' AND exec_start.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND exec_start.STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_staging_delay DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average resource_waiting_queue_time, average file_transfer_queue_time, average ratio of resource_waiting_queue_time to total_queue_time, average ratio of file_transfer_queue_time to total_queue_time, and total job count — revealing whether queuing delays at each site are dominated by resource contention or by data-staging bottlenecks?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_resource_wait_fraction, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_file_transfer_fraction, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_resource_waiting_queue_time DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by file name, what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average transfer duration, average bandwidth, and average link_load — identifying the most heavily replicated or distributed files across the grid and their associated network performance characteristics?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY transfer_count DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by file size tier (tiny: below 10000000 bytes, small: 10000000 to below 1000000000 bytes, large: 1000000000 bytes and above), what is the average and maximum disk_write_bw, average write duration, average site_storage_util, average grid_storage_util, and total write count — revealing how disk write performance and storage pressure vary with the size of data being committed?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 10000000 THEN 'tiny' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'small' ELSE 'large' END AS size_tier, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, COUNT(*) AS write_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY size_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL))

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by retry count bucket (none: 0 retries, few: 1 to 2 retries, many: 3 or more retries), what is the average and maximum cost, average execution duration, average flops, average total_queue_time, and job count — revealing how job failures and re-executions inflate monetary cost, elapsed time, and pre-execution waiting across the simulation?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 'none_0' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) <= 2 THEN 'few_1_to_2' ELSE 'many_3_plus' END AS retry_bucket, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS max_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY retry_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER))

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by file name, what is the total read count, total bytes read, count of distinct sites that accessed the file, count of distinct hosts that accessed the file, average disk_read_bw, and average read duration — identifying the most popular input files and whether their access is broadly distributed across sites or concentrated on a small number of nodes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by site_storage_util tier (low: below 0.3, medium: 0.3 to below 0.7, high: 0.7 and above), what is the average and maximum disk_write_bw, average write duration, average file size in bytes, and total write count — revealing whether high storage utilization measurably degrades disk write bandwidth and inflates write completion times?
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.3 THEN 'low'
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.7 THEN 'medium'
    ELSE 'high'
  END AS storage_util_tier,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw,
  MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_write_bw,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes,
  COUNT(*) AS total_write_count
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY storage_util_tier
ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL))

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average and maximum flops per core (flops divided by cores), average duration per core (duration divided by cores), average core count, and total job count — revealing which sites achieve the highest per-core computational throughput and most efficiently utilise their parallel CPU resources?
sql:
SELECT
  json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_core,
  MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS max_flops_per_core,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_per_core,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_core_count,
  COUNT(*) AS total_job_count
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY site
ORDER BY avg_flops_per_core DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events bucketed into 5 equal simulation-time windows, what is the average bandwidth, average link_load, average transfer duration, total bytes transferred, and transfer count per window — revealing how network utilization and transfer performance evolve as the simulation progresses from start to finish?
sql:
WITH bounds AS (
  SELECT MIN(TIME) AS t_min, MAX(TIME) AS t_max
  FROM EVENTS
  WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
)
SELECT
  MIN(CAST((e.TIME - b.t_min) * 5.0 / (b.t_max - b.t_min + 1e-9) AS INTEGER), 4) AS time_window,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration,
  SUM(CAST(json_extract(e.METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred,
  COUNT(*) AS transfer_count
FROM EVENTS e, bounds b
WHERE e.EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND e.STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY time_window
ORDER BY time_window

question: For started FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the average and maximum site_storage_util and grid_storage_util observed at the moment transfers arrive, the average file size in bytes, and the total inbound transfer count — revealing which destination sites face the heaviest pre-existing storage pressure when accepting incoming data from the grid?
sql:
SELECT
  json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util,
  MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util,
  MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes,
  COUNT(*) AS inbound_transfer_count
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started'
GROUP BY destination_site
ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events, comparing transfers where the source and destination site are the same (intra-site) against those crossing site boundaries (inter-site), what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average transfer duration, average bandwidth, and average link_load for each category — revealing the volume, speed, and congestion characteristics of local versus cross-site data movement across the simulation?
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') = json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') THEN 'intra-site'
    ELSE 'inter-site'
  END AS transfer_scope,
  COUNT(*) AS transfer_count,
  SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY transfer_scope

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the total cost, average cost per job, maximum cost for a single job, minimum cost, and total job count — revealing which sites incur the greatest aggregate monetary expenditure and whether per-site costs are driven by a few expensive outlier jobs or by broadly elevated averages?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS max_cost, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS min_cost, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_cost DESC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by file size tier (tiny: below 10000000 bytes, small: 10000000 to below 1000000000 bytes, large: 1000000000 bytes and above), what is the average and maximum disk_read_bw, average read duration, average file size in bytes, and total read count — revealing how disk read bandwidth and completion time scale with the amount of data being read?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 10000000 THEN 'tiny' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'small' ELSE 'large' END AS size_tier, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, COUNT(*) AS read_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY size_tier ORDER BY avg_file_size_bytes

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by core count, what is the average and maximum file_transfer_queue_time, average resource_waiting_queue_time, average execution duration, and total job count — revealing whether jobs requesting more parallel cores systematically face longer data-staging waits or heavier resource-contention delays before execution?
sql: SELECT CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS max_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores ORDER BY cores

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the count of high-congestion transfers where link_load exceeds 0.8, total transfer count, fraction of transfers that are high-congestion, average bandwidth, and average transfer duration — revealing which destination sites most frequently receive data over heavily loaded network links and whether congestion measurably inflates transfer times?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_congestion_count, COUNT(*) AS total_transfer_count, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS high_congestion_fraction, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY high_congestion_fraction DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by site, what is the average and maximum disk_write_bw, average site_storage_util at write completion, average write duration, total bytes written, and total write operation count — revealing per-site disk write throughput capacity and whether sites operating under higher storage utilization exhibit degraded write bandwidth?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, COUNT(*) AS write_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed JobAllocation events joined with their corresponding Started events by JOB_ID and grouped by site, what is the average, minimum, and maximum allocation phase duration (Finished TIME minus Started TIME) and total allocation count — revealing which sites incur the longest resource-allocation latency before jobs can begin staging or execution?
sql: SELECT json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_duration_s, MIN(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS min_allocation_duration_s, MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_duration_s FROM EVENTS f JOIN EVENTS s ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND s.STATE = 'Started' WHERE f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site') ORDER BY avg_allocation_duration_s DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average ratio of total_io_read_time to execution duration (the IO-bound fraction), average total_io_read_time, average execution duration, and total job count — revealing which sites run predominantly IO-bound workloads versus compute-bound workloads across the simulation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_io_fraction, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_io_read_time_s, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration_s FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') ORDER BY avg_io_fraction DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by file size tier (tiny: below 10000000 bytes, small: 10000000 to below 1000000000 bytes, large: 1000000000 bytes and above), what is the average and maximum transfer duration, average reported bandwidth, average actual throughput (size divided by duration), average link_load, and total transfer count — revealing how file size influences effective network utilisation and transfer completion time?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 10000000 THEN 'tiny' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'small' ELSE 'large' END AS size_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_s, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration_s, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_reported_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY size_tier ORDER BY avg_file_size_bytes

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average flops delivered per unit of cost (flops divided by cost), average cost per flop (cost divided by flops), average job cost, average flops, and total job count — revealing which sites provide the greatest computational value per monetary unit and which are the least cost-efficient?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_cost_unit, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_flop, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_job_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') ORDER BY avg_flops_per_cost_unit DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by host, what is the total write operation count, total bytes written, average and maximum disk_write_bw, average write duration, average site_storage_util, and average grid_storage_util — identifying which individual compute hosts bear the heaviest disk write workloads and whether those hosts operate under elevated storage pressure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_s, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the average and maximum link_load, average actual throughput (size divided by duration in bytes per second), average reported bandwidth, and total transfer count — revealing which sites generate the most network-congested outbound data flows and how much their effective throughput deviates from their reported bandwidth?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_reported_bandwidth, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_link_load DESC

question: For each site, combining completed FileRead and FileWrite events, what is the total bytes read, total bytes written, read operation count, write operation count, and the ratio of bytes read to bytes written — revealing whether each site's storage workload is predominantly read-heavy or write-heavy and how disk access patterns differ across the grid?
sql: SELECT site, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_count, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN bytes ELSE 0 END), 0) AS read_write_byte_ratio FROM (SELECT 'FileRead' AS event_type, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS bytes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' UNION ALL SELECT 'FileWrite' AS event_type, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS bytes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished') combined GROUP BY site ORDER BY read_write_byte_ratio DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events bucketed by link_load tier (light: below 0.3, moderate: 0.3 to below 0.7, heavy: 0.7 and above), what is the average and maximum transfer duration, average file size, average reported bandwidth, average actual throughput (size divided by duration), and transfer count — revealing how increasing link congestion degrades effective data movement performance across the simulation?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.3 THEN 'light' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.7 THEN 'moderate' ELSE 'heavy' END AS link_load_tier, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_reported_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY link_load_tier ORDER BY CASE link_load_tier WHEN 'light' THEN 1 WHEN 'moderate' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by individual host, what is the total job count, average and maximum execution duration, average flops, average cost, and average grid_cpu_util at completion — revealing how compute workloads are distributed across individual machines and which hosts process the heaviest or most expensive jobs?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host ORDER BY job_count DESC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by site, what is the count of distinct files accessed, count of distinct hosts that performed reads, total bytes read, average and maximum disk_read_bw, and total read operation count — revealing the breadth of file access and aggregate read throughput at each site and whether read bandwidth is consistent or highly variable across a site's hosts?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_read_bw, COUNT(*) AS total_read_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events bucketed by reported bandwidth tier (low: below 1,000,000,000 bytes/s, medium: 1,000,000,000 to below 10,000,000,000 bytes/s, high: 10,000,000,000 bytes/s and above), what is the transfer count, average and maximum transfer duration, average actual throughput (size divided by duration), average latency, average link_load, and total bytes transferred — revealing whether higher-capacity links achieve proportionally better effective throughput or whether congestion and latency impose diminishing returns?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 10000000000 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS bandwidth_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY bandwidth_tier ORDER BY CASE bandwidth_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For started JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average and maximum site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util at the moment of job launch, the count of jobs launched when grid_cpu_util exceeds 0.8, total job count, and the fraction of launches occurring under high global CPU load — revealing which sites most frequently dispatch compute work into an already heavily loaded grid and whether local versus global CPU pressure differs across sites?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_launch, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_cpu_util_at_launch, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_launch, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_cpu_util_at_launch, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_load_launches, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS high_load_launch_fraction FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_grid_cpu_util_at_launch DESC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by host and site, what is the total read operation count, count of distinct files read, count of distinct disks used, total bytes read, average and maximum disk_read_bw, and average read duration — identifying which individual compute hosts perform the heaviest disk read workloads, how broadly their reads span different files and storage devices, and how per-host read throughput varies within and across sites?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_operation_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_read, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks_used, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average, minimum, and maximum execution duration, the ratio of maximum to average duration as an outlier-severity indicator, average cost, and total job count — revealing which sites produce the most extreme execution time outliers relative to their own baseline and whether anomalously long-running jobs also incur disproportionately high monetary costs?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS min_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_to_avg_duration_ratio, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY max_to_avg_duration_ratio DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by file name, what is the write count, count of distinct sites where the file was written, count of distinct disks used, total bytes written, average disk_write_bw, and average write duration — identifying which output files are most frequently committed to storage, whether they are broadly distributed across many sites and disks or concentrated on a few storage nodes, and how write performance compares across files with different distribution patterns?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY write_count DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by the ordered site pair (source_site → destination_site), what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average transfer duration, average bandwidth, and average link_load — revealing which specific inter-site corridors carry the most data and exhibit the highest network congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site'), json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') ORDER BY total_bytes_transferred DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site_cpu_util tier at job completion (low: below 0.3, medium: 0.3 to below 0.7, high: 0.7 and above), what is the average execution duration, average cost, average flops, average grid_cpu_util, and total job count — revealing whether jobs finishing under heavier local CPU pressure exhibit longer durations or higher costs than those completing under lighter load?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) < 0.3 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) < 0.7 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS site_cpu_util_tier, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, COUNT(*) AS job_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site_cpu_util_tier ORDER BY CASE site_cpu_util_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by disk, what is the total read operation count, count of distinct files read, count of distinct hosts using the disk, total bytes read, average disk_read_bw, and average read duration — identifying which individual storage disks are most heavily utilized for reads and whether disks shared across many hosts show degraded per-read throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by disk, what is the total write operation count, count of distinct files written, count of distinct hosts using that disk, total bytes written, average disk_write_bw, and average site_storage_util at write completion — identifying which individual disks bear the heaviest write workloads and whether disks accessed by many hosts operate under elevated storage utilization?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk ORDER BY write_count DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by latency tier (low: below 0.001 seconds, medium: 0.001 to below 0.01 seconds, high: 0.01 seconds and above), what is the transfer count, average and maximum transfer duration, average reported bandwidth, average actual throughput (size divided by duration), and average link_load — revealing how network latency independently shapes transfer completion time and effective data movement speed across the simulation?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.001 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.01 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY CASE latency_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the count of jobs that required at least one retry, the total job count, the fraction of jobs that needed retries, the average retry count among only those jobs that did retry, and the maximum retries observed — revealing which sites suffer the most job instability and how severe retry behaviour is at each location?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_with_retries, ROUND(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 4) AS retry_fraction, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_retries_when_retried, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY retry_fraction DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average file_transfer_queue_time, average resource_waiting_queue_time, average total_queue_time, the fraction of total queue time attributable to file-transfer waits, and the fraction attributable to resource-contention waits — revealing whether each site's scheduling bottleneck is primarily driven by data-staging delays or by compute resource scarcity?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)), 0) AS file_transfer_fraction, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)), 0) AS resource_waiting_fraction FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_total_queue_time DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events bucketed by retry count (no retries, exactly one retry, two or more retries), what is the job count, average execution duration, average cost, average total_queue_time, and average flops — revealing whether jobs that required retries are systematically more expensive, slower, and more queue-intensive than first-attempt successes?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN 'no_retries' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN 'one_retry' ELSE 'two_or_more_retries' END AS retry_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY retry_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER))

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by individual file, what is the total read count, count of distinct sites that read the file, count of distinct hosts that read it, total bytes read, average disk_read_bw, and average read duration — revealing which files are most broadly replicated and accessed across the grid and whether high-demand files maintain consistent read throughput regardless of which site serves them?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average site_cpu_util, average grid_cpu_util, the average difference of site_cpu_util minus grid_cpu_util, the count of jobs where the site runs more than 0.2 utilization units hotter than the global grid, and the total job count — revealing which sites are systematically over-loaded relative to the rest of the grid and whether local CPU pressure is decoupled from global grid-wide load?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_minus_grid_cpu_util, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) > 0.2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS jobs_site_hotter_than_grid FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_minus_grid_cpu_util DESC
